MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-31-2013 Set For Trial On Nov-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Benjamin C. Koodrich, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788.1900, for cross defendants and moving party;
Christopher Thomas, Esq. of the Law Offices of Richard Wahng, Tel. 510.491.5021, for defendants/cross complainants.

RULING: Counsel for Cross Complainant requested for a continuance of the hearing, which was denied. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT DANIEL LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The court grants and denies the motion for summary judgment and adjudication in part. The moving party has maintained its summary judgment burden on its cross-complaint. The declaration of Daniel Lee shows that MSW owes $41,046.23. In opposition, the declaration of Ms. Wong (MSW's former president) creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the moving party properly performed the accounting services. Therefore, the motion is denied as to issues 1-5. The court grants the motion for summary adjudication as to issues 6-20. MSW's responses to the moving party's state all facts interrogatories are factually devoid. MSW merely parrots the allegations of the complaint. In opposition, MSW fails to create a triable issue of material fact. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: WILLIAM TAYLOR, ESQ., 415.777.1688, FOR PLAINTIFFS RYAN MAK AND NORRIS MAK.
CHRISTOPHER THOMAS, ESQ., 510.491.5021, FOR DEFENDANT M.S.W. ENTERPRISES, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS RYAN MAK, NORRIS MAK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. Plaintiffs have met their prima facie burden by setting forth evidence establishing the following elements: (1) Plaintiffs and Defendant entered into contracts by executing the nine promissory notes (Ryan Mak Decl. 2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22, 22, 26, 30, Exs. A, C-I; Norris Mak Decl., 2, Ex. A); (2) Plaintiffs performed by loaning the money (Ryan Mak Decl., 3, 7, 11, 15, 19, 23, 27, 31; Norris Mak Decl. 3); (3) Plaintiffs demanded payment (Ryan Mak Decl., 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, Ex. B; Norris Mak Decl. 4, Ex. B); and (4) Defendant breached the terms of the note by not paying on demand (Ryan Mak Decl., 5, 9, 13, 17, 21, 25, 29, 33; Norris Mak Decl., 5). Therefore, the burden shifts to Defendant to raise a triable issue of material fact. (CCP sec. 437(c)(p)(1).) Defendant has not met its burden. First, the claims as to the 2005 promissory note are not barred by the statute of limitations. (Com. Code sec. 3118(b).) Second, both declarations submitted in support of the opposition are not dated, as required by CCP sec. 2015.5. Even if the Court considers the defective declarations, the declarations do not give rise to a triable issue of material fact. "When opposition to a motion for summary judgment is based on inferences, those inferences must be reasonably deducible from the evidence, and not such as are derived from speculation, conjecture, imagination, or guesswork." (Joseph E. Di Loreto, Inc. v. O'Neill (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 149, 161.) Defendant provides no evidence to give rise to the inference that Plaintiffs fraudulently induced Defendant to execute the promissory note. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Continued To May-13-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS RYAN MAK AND NORRIS MAK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is continued on the court's own motion to May 13, 2014 in order to permit Plaintiffs the opportunity to reply to Defendants' opposition that was late-filed and mail-served on March 24, 2014. (See CCP 437c(b)(2).) Any reply shall be filed and served in accordance with CCP 437c(b)(4). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
